title: Comparative thermo-electrochemical study of lignin- and starch-derived carbon electrodes modified with Zn(TFSI)2 and ionic liquids for lithium-ion battery applications
abstract_summary: This study presents a comparative electrochemical investigation of carbon anodes derived from kraft lignin and wheat starch, modified via hydrothermal treatments or ionic liquids. The introduction of Zn(TFSI)2 onto lignin-derived carbon (LKN2H) successfully incorporates sulfonyl and zinc-ion functional groups on the surface, which significantly lowers the charge-transfer resistance (<300 Ohm), maximizes ionic conductivity (0.316 S/m), and achieves an exceptionally low activation energy (15.3 kJ/mol) for lithium-ion diffusion.
green_strategy: Utilization of abundant, renewable biopolymer waste (kraft lignin and starch) as precursors for manufacturing sustainable carbon anodes, replacing fossil-based synthetic graphite.
fabrication_method: Carbonization of biopolymers under nitrogen (900 °C for lignin, 600 °C for starch), followed by hydrothermal modification with Zn(TFSI)2 dissolved in distilled water at 140 °C for 24 h in a Teflon-lined autoclave.

performance_metrics.comparison_baseline: 92.3 kJ mol^-1 (for unmodified lignin-derived carbon LKN2)

limitations: Lignin carbon pre-treated with protic ionic liquid [C4Him][HSO4] (LK-IL 1) suffered from aggressive surface degradation, pore blockage, and highly elevated charge-transfer resistance (>600 Ohm).
Wheat starch-derived carbon modified with Zn(TFSI)2 (starchN2H) exhibited poor performance relative to lignin, displaying high activation energy (90.3 kJ/mol) and thermodynamic instability.
High thermal processing temperatures (up to 900 °C) are still required to carbonize the biopolymer precursors under an inert atmosphere.

figure_or_table_evidence.evidence_summary: Summarizes average kinetic and thermodynamic parameters (Ed, A, k, dH, dG, dS, K) across all tested biocarbon electrodes, highlighting the superior transport properties of LKN2H.
Provides a concise summary of the key electrochemical parameters including charge-transfer resistance (Rct) and activation energy (Ed) across the studied modified biocarbons.
Presents Nyquist plots for LKN2, LKN2H, LK-IL 1, LK-IL 2, and starchN2H showing distinct temperature-dependent changes in interfacial charge-transfer arcs.

candidate_research_claims.claim_text: Hydrothermal modification of kraft lignin-derived carbon using Zn(TFSI)2 significantly enhances lithium-ion transport kinetics and decreases charge-transfer resistance by introducing active zinc-ion and sulfonyl coordination sites, resulting in an exceptionally low activation energy of 15.3 kJ/mol.
candidate_research_claims.experimental_condition: Carbonized lignin at 900 °C modified with Zn(TFSI)2 under hydrothermal conditions at 140 °C for 24 h
candidate_research_claims.limitation: The efficacy is heavily dependent on the biopolymer precursor chemistry, as starch-derived carbons did not exhibit comparable kinetic improvements.
candidate_research_claims.citation_text: Notably, the Zn(TFSI)2-modified lignin-based carbon (LKN2H) exhibited the most favorable electrochemical properties, including the highest ionic conductivity and the lowest charge transfer resistance. This enhancement is attributed to the introduction of sulfonyl and Zn2+ coordination sites that facilitate ion transport.
